preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 62%, leaving South Africa with a 13% win probability and a draw at 25%. South Korea enters as a clear statistical favorite, though the underdog's chances remain credible within a neutral venue framework.

The spread across outcomes shows moderate separation. A South Korea victory is the likeliest single outcome, but the combined 39% probability of either a draw or South African win indicates the match carries meaningful competitive uncertainty. The draw probability near one-quarter suggests the teams are not dramatically mismatched in a way that rules out a stalemate result.

The sportsbook consensus diverges meaningfully from the model. Books price South Korea lower at 48%, assign a modestly higher draw probability at 28%, and rate South Africa substantially higher at 24%—a gap of roughly 11 percentage points in South Africa's favor compared to the model. Polymarket prices all three outcomes equally at 33% each, signaling high uncertainty among those traders. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; they reflect different information sets and methodologies.
